Aging Levels define the time buckets used in AR aging reports to categorise outstanding balances by how long they have been overdue.
Payment Types define the methods guests can use to pay their folio balance (e.g. Cash, Credit Card, Bank Transfer). They appear in the Payment modal in Cashiering → Transaction and Quick Checkout.

Routing Codes define how charges are routed from a guest's folio to another payer (e.g. company, travel agent, or another room). Each routing code specifies which transaction codes are included in the routing.
- Transaction code group — High-level grouping (e.g. Room, F&B, Tax).

Account Receivables Setup contains the reference data used for managing accounts receivable — credit accounts for companies, travel agents, and other payers. Access via Configuration → Account Receivables.
Restrict Reasons are the reasons used when placing a restriction or block on an AR account — for example, when a company has exceeded its credit limit or has an overdue balance.
Use this checklist when setting up a new property (new database) in Hotelium PMS. Follow the steps in order — some items depend on others being created first.
Reminder Cycles define how often payment reminders are sent to AR accounts with outstanding balances.

Cash Registers are the logical registers used for cashier login. Each cashier session is tied to a register. Before using any cashiering screen (Transaction, Cashier Shift, Quick Checkout), a user must log in to a cash register.
Configure property details:
Service Charges define automatic charges that are added on top of eligible billing items. For example, a 10% service charge applied to all room charges, or a 5% tax on food & beverage.
Cashiers link user accounts to cash registers, enabling those users to log in to a cash register and use cashiering screens.
Transaction Codes define every type of charge, payment, and adjustment that can be posted to a guest folio. Each code has a short code, name, group, and a set of flags that control how it behaves.
- Add / edit articles — Items or services (e.g. minibar, laundry) used for POS-style charges.

Services are additional offerings your property provides to guests — such as airport transfers, laundry, spa, or room service. Configuring services allows them to be selected and tracked in reservations and housekeeping.
Departments are used for reporting or access control:
Cashiering setup includes transaction codes, transaction code groups/sub-groups, payment types, routing codes, cashiers, articles, exchange rates, and service charges.
If the product uses a license or activation:
General setup covers hotel information, system date, users, user groups, departments, job titles, license, cash register setup, TrackIt, and transportation types.
If cashiering uses separate exchange rates:
Track It is the item-tracking system used for Lost & Found in Housekeeping. It organises items using four reference tables — each has its own configuration page.
The system (business) date is the "today" used for reservations, rates, and reporting when different from the real date.
Job titles are used for users or profiles:
- Transaction code sub-group — Finer grouping within a group.
Track It Types are sub-categories within a Track It Group. They describe the specific kind of item (e.g. Phone within Electronics).
Users are the staff accounts that log in to the PMS. Each user has a username, password, assigned permissions (directly or via a user group), and optional profile details.
Account Types categorise AR (accounts receivable) accounts. Examples: Corporate, Travel Agent, Government, Group.
- Add / edit out-of-order reasons (e.g. Repair, Renovation)

User Groups define reusable permission sets. Assign a group to a user and they inherit all permissions in that group. This is the recommended way to manage permissions for multiple staff with the same role.
- Add / edit housekeeping sections (e.g. Floor 1, Tower A)
Room Maintenance Reasons are the reasons used when logging a maintenance issue for a room. They appear in the Maintenance tab of Housekeeping Management.
Track It Actions describe what happened to a lost or found item — the action taken by staff. The Category flag distinguishes between a claim action and a lost/found action.
Track It Locations define where a lost or found item was discovered or is being stored. Staff select a location when registering a lost & found item.

Configuration is where you set up all reference data and options used across the PMS: rooms, rates, cashiering, profiles, housekeeping, and general settings. Access and visibility depend on user permissions.
Track It Groups are the top-level categories for items in the Lost & Found system. Every item registered in Lost & Found must belong to a group.
- Add / edit attendances (e.g. attendants or teams)
Housekeeping setup includes housekeeping sections, attendances, tasks, room management, lost & found reasons, management types, and out-of-order reasons.
Cities for address.
Management Types classify maintenance records — they describe the type or category of a maintenance job. Used alongside Room Maintenance Reasons for more detailed tracking and reporting.

Profile setup includes reference data used in individual, company, and travel agent profiles: preferences, VIP levels, loyalty tiers, titles, languages, nationalities, regions, countries, territories, states, districts, cities, postal codes, and inactive reason.
- Add / edit housekeeping task types (e.g. Clean, Deep Clean, VIP Setup)
Countries for address and reporting.
Postal or ZIP codes.
Languages offered or stored for profiles.
Regions (e.g. for address or reporting).
If transportation types are used (e.g. for pickup or guest transport):
Salutations or titles (e.g. Mr, Ms, Dr).
Reasons for marking a profile or rate inactive.
- Add / edit lost & found reasons (e.g. Left in room, Lobby, Restaurant)
Guest or profile preferences (e.g. bed type, floor, newspaper).
Districts within a state or city, if used.
Loyalty program tiers.
Currencies defines the currencies your property accepts for billing and payments. The base currency is used for all internal calculations; other currencies are converted via exchange rates.

Packages bundle a room rate with additional inclusions (e.g. breakfast, airport transfer, spa credit). When a package rate is selected on a reservation, the included items are posted to the folio automatically.
Nationalities used in profile and reporting.
States or provinces.
Rate Categories are a finer grouping within a Rate Class (e.g. Weekday, Weekend, Peak Season within the BAR class).

Market Groups are the top-level categories that group individual market codes. For example, a market group "Corporate" might contain market codes like "Corporate Negotiated", "Corporate Rack", and "Corporate Government".
VIP or loyalty levels used when classifying guests.
Territories within a country, if used.
Source Groups are the top-level categories that group individual source codes. For example, a source group "Online" might contain source codes like "Booking.com", "Expedia", and "Hotel Website".
Sources (also called Source Codes) identify where a reservation came from — the booking channel or origin. Each source belongs to a Source Group.
Cancellation Reasons are required when cancelling a reservation. They provide a record of why the booking was cancelled for reporting and auditing purposes.
Rate Classes are the broadest grouping of rates (e.g. BAR, Corporate, Package). They sit above Rate Categories in the hierarchy.
- Discount reason — Reason for a discount.
- Add / edit rates
Exchange Rates define the conversion rate between foreign currencies and your base currency. They are used when guests pay in a foreign currency or when rates are quoted in a non-base currency.
- Reservation lost reason — Why a reservation was lost.
- Origin code — Origin of the reservation (e.g. region, campaign).
- Booking type — e.g. Guaranteed, Non-guaranteed.
Purpose of Stay codes classify why a guest is visiting — for example, Business, Leisure, Conference, or Transit. They are used on reservations for segmentation and reporting.
- Market — Market segment or channel (e.g. Direct, OTA).
Inactive Reasons are used when deactivating a reservation, profile, or rate — they record why the record was made inactive. This provides an audit trail for deactivations.
- Alert code — Alerts or flags on the reservation.
Floor Plans define the floors of your property. Rooms are assigned to a floor, which controls how they are grouped in the Room Plan, Room Chart, and housekeeping views.

Reservation setup includes room types, room classes, rooms, reservation types, reservation methods, booking types, business segments, floor plans, special requests, room status, room move, trace/origin/alert codes, discount/cancel/waitlist/lost/refused reasons, and inactive reasons.
Rate setup covers rates, rate setup, rate classes, rate categories, markets, sources, packages, currencies, and exchange rates.
Rooms are the individual sellable units in the property. Each room belongs to a room type and floor, and is linked to a hotel in multi-property setups.
Visa Types are the categories of visas that guests may hold — for example, Tourist, Business, Transit, Diplomatic. They are stored on individual guest profiles for immigration and reporting purposes.
Reservation Types classify bookings and control which fields are required when creating a reservation. Examples: Regular, Walk-In, Group, Complimentary, House Use.
- Business segment — Market segment (e.g. Corporate, Leisure).
- Trace code — Internal trace or follow-up.
- Room status — Statuses used for room state (e.g. Available, Out of Order). May overlap with housekeeping status.
- Reservation refused reason — Why a reservation was refused.
This page covers profile details (viewing and editing your user profile) and changing your password.
- Room class — Broad category (e.g. Standard, Deluxe).
This page lists major updates to the Hotelium PMS user manual.
The PMS shows notifications in the top bar via a bell icon. Use them to stay informed about housekeeping, reservations, and billing without leaving your current screen.

What you see and can do in the PMS depends on your user permissions. Permissions are assigned by your administrator through user groups or direct user rights. This page summarises which menu areas typically require which access.
Room Types define the categories of rooms available for sale (e.g. Standard King, Deluxe Twin, Suite). Each room type belongs to a room class and is used in reservations, rates, and availability.
- Waitlist reason — Reason or type for waitlist.
- Special requests — e.g. High floor, Late checkout.

The Billing module is the full folio management workspace for a reservation. It shows all charges, payments, and folio details for a selected reservation, and lets you post charges, take payments, split or transfer items, adjust amounts, void transactions, and print folios.

Welcome to the Hotelium Property Management System (PMS) user manual. This guide explains how to use the PMS application for daily hotel operations, from login and dashboard to reservations, front desk, cashiering, housekeeping, night audit, and system configuration.

Journal by Transaction provides a detailed view of all billing items and payments posted within a cash register session or date range. It is used to audit and reconcile transactions across reservations, cashiers, and room types.
Cashiering handles guest folio billing, payments, and cashier sessions. Access usually requires Cash Register Login before using Transaction, Cashier Shift, or Quick Checkout.

Room Routing (also called Room Routing History) shows all routing rules that have been set up across reservations for a selected date range. It is used to review and audit how charges are being routed between rooms, folios, or payers.
Common issues and what to do when something goes wrong.
- Reservation method — e.g. Phone, Web, Walk-in.
Quick reference for terms used in the Hotelium PMS manual.

Adding screenshots to the manual makes it easier for users to follow. This page lists recommended screenshots and where to use them. Save images under static/img/docs/ and reference them in the docs as /img/docs/.png.
Cashier Shift is used to manage cash register sessions — view shift history, close a shift (shift drop), and run cashier reports. It is the reconciliation hub before night audit.
Short guide to finding your way and using the Hotelium PMS manual.

Front Desk covers the daily front-office workflow: room planning, in-house guest management, and checked-out guest history. It is the central hub for check-in, check-out, room moves, and quick folio access.

Cash Register Login is required before using any cashiering function (Transaction, Cashier Shift, Quick Checkout). It links your user session to a specific cash register so all transactions are tracked per register.
The Dashboard is the home screen after login. It shows an overview of your property's status and quick links to common tasks.

The Lost & Found tab (also called Track It) is used to register found items, track their status, and record when they are returned or disposed of. It uses the Track It system which categorises items by group, type, action, and location.

The Database Manager allows administrators to manage multiple hotel databases (tenants) from a single PMS installation. It is used to create, back up, restore, and delete property databases, and to switch between properties at login.

The Checked-Out list shows all reservations that have been checked out. Use it to review departure history, open folios for post-checkout adjustments, and move reservations back to in-house if a checkout was made in error.

The Transaction screen is the main cashiering workspace. It shows all in-house reservations on the left and the selected reservation's folio detail on the right. Use it to post charges, take payments, void transactions, print folios, and check out guests.
User Activity Logs record actions performed by users in the PMS — logins, reservation changes, payments, configuration changes, and more. Use them for auditing, troubleshooting, and compliance.

Quick Checkout speeds up the departure process by letting you process multiple reservations in sequence — auto-posting room charges, taking payment, and checking out — without opening each folio individually.

The Housekeeping module is used to track room status, assign tasks, manage lost & found, maintenance, and out-of-order rooms. In the app, the main screen is Housekeeping Management, with tabs for each area.
The Room Status tab shows the current housekeeping status of every room. Use it to see which rooms are ready to sell, need cleaning, or are out of order — and to update statuses in bulk.
The In-House list shows all reservations currently checked in. Use it to find guests, open folios, process check-outs, move rooms, and manage alerts, comments, and messages.

The Out of Order tab manages rooms that are taken out of sale — either fully out of order (OOO) or temporarily out of service (OOS). Out of order rooms are excluded from availability and shown with a special status in Room Status, Room Plan, and Room Chart.
The Maintenance tab is used to log and track maintenance tasks for rooms that are out of order. Each maintenance record is linked to an out-of-order room and assigned to engineering staff.
The Tasks tab is used to create, assign, update, and complete housekeeping tasks. Tasks are linked to specific rooms and assigned to housekeeping staff.

The Housekeeping Room Plan is a calendar-style grid showing room availability and occupancy over a date range. It is used to visualise which rooms are occupied, vacant, or blocked, and to create new reservations directly from the grid.
Company Profile stores data for companies used as payers, group organisers, or corporate guests in reservations and billing.

Night Audit is the end-of-day process that closes the business date, posts room charges, and produces audit reports. It is run once per property per business day, after all front-office and cashier activity is complete.
- Room move — Reasons for moving a guest to another room.

The Room Plan is a calendar-style grid showing all rooms and their occupancy across a date range. Use it to see availability at a glance, create new reservations by clicking on a room/date cell, and navigate to existing reservations.

Operations covers the day-to-day use of the PMS: reservations, front desk, cashiering (folio, cash register), housekeeping, night audit, and reports. Use this section to find the right screen and workflow for each task.
The Housekeeping Report shows the current room status across the property, along with optional sections for out-of-order rooms, maintenance records, lost & found records, and housekeeping tasks.
Housekeeping Management is the main housekeeping screen. It is a tabbed interface that brings together room status, tasks, lost & found, maintenance, and out-of-order management in one place.
Profiles store guest and payer information used in reservations and billing. The PMS supports three profile types:
The Frontdesk Report shows a snapshot of front-desk activity for a selected business date: arrivals, departures, in-house guests, and room status.
Travel Agent Profile stores data for travel agents and agencies used in reservations for billing routing and commission tracking.

The Night Audit Report shows a list of all audit runs with their key metrics, and lets you drill into the details of a specific run — revenue summary, payment summary, reservation summary, and occupancy statistics.
The Billing Report shows all billing items (charges) and payments for a selected date range, with a summary of total charges, total payments, and outstanding balance.
The Guest Information Report shows all in-house guests for a selected business date, including full profile details for both primary and additional guests on each reservation.
The Reservation Report shows all reservations matching the selected filters, with a summary of totals and breakdowns by market, source, and room type.

The Room Chart gives a visual, floor-by-floor snapshot of all rooms and their current status for a selected date. It is used for quick room assignment, walk-in check-ins, and housekeeping status review.

The Manager Report is a consolidated daily summary for management, built from a night audit run. It shows occupancy, revenue, payments, outstanding balances, and breakdowns by transaction code, payment type, and room type.

The Reservation Confirmation Letter is a printable document sent to the guest to confirm their booking. It shows the reservation details, stay dates, room type, rate, and property contact information.
Post Room Charge manually posts room revenue for a specific reservation and date. Use it when room charges were not posted automatically by night audit, or to post charges for a specific past date.
The Trial Balance Report aggregates the night audit guest ledger by transaction code for a selected audit run, showing total debits, total credits, and the net balance per code.

The Registration Form (also called the registration card) is a printable guest check-in document. It contains the guest's personal details, stay information, and a signature line for the guest to sign at check-in.
The Departures view shows all in-house reservations with a departure date on the selected date. Use it to prepare folios, process check-outs, and hand rooms back to housekeeping.
This guide walks through the full checkout process — from verifying the folio to checking out the guest and updating room status.
The Reservation module is used to create, view, edit, and manage guest reservations. It connects to guest profiles, rooms, rates, billing, and housekeeping.
The Waitlist holds reservation requests when no suitable room or rate is available. When space opens, convert the entry to a full reservation.
The Arrivals view shows all reservations with an arrival date on the selected date. Use it to prepare for check-ins, confirm room readiness, and process arrivals efficiently.

The Report module provides predefined reports for reservations, front desk, billing (including folio summary), housekeeping, and night audit. Use it to run folio, cash register, and night audit reports from one place. In the app, Report is a menu with categories you can choose.
Individual Profile stores personal data for guests and contacts used in reservations. It is the most detailed profile type, with three sections: Personal Info, Address, and Travel Documents.

This page describes two common end-to-end workflows: check-in (from reservation to in-house) and end-of-day cashier and night audit. Use them as reference when training staff or writing local procedures.
Guidelines for keeping the Hotelium PMS manual buildable, link-valid, and consistent.
The Cancellations view lists all cancelled reservations. Use it to review cancellation history, filter by reason or date, and manage cancellation records.
The All Reservations list shows every booking. Use it to search, filter, create, edit, and manage reservations.
This guide explains how to link an individual, company, or travel agent profile to a reservation — either as the primary guest, payer, or additional guest.
documents.s: Linking a Profile to a Reservation

documents.t: Follow these steps in order to see the main flow of the PMS: login → dashboard → one reservation → one check-in → one folio. Use this path when you are new to the system or training staff.
Aging Levels define the time buckets used in AR aging reports to categorise outstanding balances by how long they have been overdue.
Configuration → Account Receivables → Aging Level

Field Description Name Label for the aging bucket (e.g. Current, 1–30 Days, 31–60 Days, 61–90 Days, Over 90 Days). Days From Start of the aging period in days. Days To End of the aging period in days. Active Whether this level is used in reports.
Aging levels appear in AR aging reports to show how long invoices or balances have been outstanding. Define at least one level for current (0 days) and additional levels for overdue periods.
Account Receivables Setup Reminder Cycles
Payment Types define the methods guests can use to pay their folio balance (e.g. Cash, Credit Card, Bank Transfer). They appear in the Payment modal in Cashiering → Transaction and Quick Checkout.
Configuration → Cashiering → Payment Type

Field Description Short Code Abbreviated code (e.g. CASH, VISA, BT). Name Full name (e.g. Cash, Visa Card, Bank Transfer). Sequence Display order in the payment modal. Active Whether this payment type is available for selection.

Payment types work alongside transaction codes. When a payment is posted, the system uses the linked transaction code to record it in the guest ledger and reports. Ensure the corresponding transaction code has the appropriate payment flag set (Cash, Credit Card, Bank Transfer, etc.).

If a payment type requires a credit card number, ensure the corresponding Transaction Code has the Credit Card flag enabled. When a reservation uses a payment type linked to a credit card transaction code, the credit card number field becomes required on the reservation form.
A payment type is not appearing in the payment modal. Check that the payment type is marked as Active. Also ensure the user has permission to post payments.
Transaction Codes Cashiering Setup Overview Cashiering → Transaction

Routing Codes define how charges are routed from a guest's folio to another payer (e.g. company, travel agent, or another room). Each routing code specifies which transaction codes are included in the routing.
Configuration → Cashiering → Routing Code

Field Description Short Code Abbreviated code (e.g. CORP, TA). Name Full name of the routing code (e.g. Corporate Billing, Travel Agent Commission). Sequence Display order. Transaction Codes The transaction codes that are routed when this routing code is applied. Multiple codes can be selected.

Routing codes are applied per reservation from Cashiering → Transaction (folio routing section). When a routing rule is set up on a reservation, charges matching the selected transaction codes are automatically routed to the target payer (company or travel agent profile). The property-wide view of all active routing rules is in Cashiering → Room Routing.
Transaction Codes Cashiering → Transaction Cashiering → Room Routing
Transaction code group — High-level grouping (e.g. Room, F&B, Tax). Used for reporting and folio layout. Access via Configuration → Cashiering (or equivalent sub-menus).

Account Receivables Setup contains the reference data used for managing accounts receivable — credit accounts for companies, travel agents, and other payers. Access via Configuration → Account Receivables.

Sub-menu Purpose Account Types Categories of AR accounts (e.g. Corporate, Travel Agent). Aging Levels Define aging buckets for outstanding balance reporting (e.g. 0–30 days, 31–60 days). Reminder Cycles Configure how often payment reminders are sent to AR accounts. Restrict Reasons Reasons used when restricting or blocking an AR account. Access requires the relevant Account Receivables setup permissions. If this menu is not visible, ask your administrator to grant access.

Cashiering Setup — Transaction codes, payment types, routing codes. Profile Setup — Company and travel agent profiles that use AR accounts. Configuration Overview — Full list of all configuration areas.
Restrict Reasons are the reasons used when placing a restriction or block on an AR account — for example, when a company has exceeded its credit limit or has an overdue balance.
Configuration → Account Receivables → Restrict Reason

Field Description Name The reason for the restriction (e.g. Overdue Balance, Credit Limit Exceeded, Account Suspended). Description Optional additional detail. Active Whether this reason is available for selection.
When restricting an AR account, the user selects a restrict reason from this list. The reason is recorded on the account and may appear in AR reports or alerts.
Account Receivables Setup Account Types

Use this checklist when setting up a new property (new database) in Hotelium PMS. Follow the steps in order — some items depend on others being created first. tip Complete each section before moving to the next. You can always come back to add more data later, but the items marked required must exist before you can create reservations or post charges.

Start here. Everything else depends on these basics. Task Where Required Enter hotel name, address, phone, email, logo Hotel Information Yes Set the opening business date System Date Yes Create departments (Front Desk, Housekeeping, Engineering, etc.) Departments Yes Create job titles Job Titles No Create user groups with correct permissions User Groups Yes Create user accounts and assign to groups Users Yes Activate the license License Yes Set up cash registers (one per cashier station) Cash Register Setup Yes Add transportation types (if used) Transportation Types No Add services (if used) Services No Set up Track It groups, types, actions, locations (for Lost & Found) Track It No

Reference data used on guest, company, and travel agent profiles. Task Where Required Add regions and countries Regions, Countries Yes Add states, districts, cities, postal codes States, Districts, Cities, Postal Codes No Add nationalities Nationalities Yes Add titles (Mr, Mrs, Ms, Dr, etc.) Titles Yes Add languages Languages No Add VIP levels VIP Levels No Add loyalty tiers Loyalty Tiers No Add visa types Visa Types No Add preferences Preferences No Add inactive reasons Inactive Reason No

Rooms and all reference data needed to create reservations. Task Where Required Create room classes Room Classes Yes Create room types Room Types Yes Create rooms (assign to room type and floor) Rooms Yes Create reservation types Reservation Types Yes Create reservation methods Reservation Methods Yes Create booking types Booking Types No Create business segments Business Segments No Add floor plans (if using visual floor plan) Floor Plans No Add special requests Special Requests No Add room status types Room Status Yes Add room move reasons Room Move No Add trace codes, origin codes, alert codes Trace Codes, Origin Codes, Alert Codes No Add cancellation, waitlist, lost, refused reasons Cancellation Reasons, Waitlist Reasons, Lost Reasons, Refused Reasons Yes Add purpose of stay codes Purpose of Stay No Add discount types Discount Reasons No

Rates and pricing. Rooms must exist before you can attach rates to room types. Task Where Required Add currencies Currencies Yes Add exchange rates (if accepting foreign currency) Exchange Rates No Add market groups and market codes Market Groups, Markets Yes Add source groups and source codes Source Groups, Sources Yes Add rate classes and rate categories Rate Classes, Rate Categories Yes Create rate plans and attach to room types Rates Yes Create packages (if offering room+breakfast, etc.) Packages No

Transaction codes and payment setup. Must be done before posting any charges. Task Where Required Create transaction code groups and sub-groups Transaction Code Groups, Sub-Groups Yes Create transaction codes (room, tax, F&B, etc.) Transaction Codes Yes Create payment types (cash, card, bank transfer, etc.) Payment Types Yes Create cashiers and link to user accounts Cashiers Yes Set up routing codes (if using charge routing) Routing Codes No Set up service charges (tax %, service charge %) Service Charges Yes Add articles (if using article-based billing) Articles No Add cashiering exchange rates Exchange Rates (Cashiering) No

Required before using the Housekeeping module. Task Where Required Create housekeeping sections Sections No Create housekeeping attendants Attendances No Create housekeeping task types Tasks No Add out-of-order/service reasons Out of Order Reasons Yes Add room maintenance reasons Room Management No Add lost & found reasons Lost & Found Reasons No Add maintenance types Management Types No

Only needed if your property uses direct billing to companies or travel agents. Task Where Required Add account types Account Types No Add aging levels Aging Levels No Add reminder cycles Reminder Cycles No Add restrict reasons Restrict Reasons No

Before going live, run through this checklist: Log in as a front desk user — can you see Reservations and Front Desk? Log in as a cashier — can you log in to a cash register and open Transaction? Create a test reservation — does it save with a room and rate? Check in the test reservation — does the status change to In-House? Post a room charge — does it appear on the folio? Post a payment — does the balance reach zero? Check out the test reservation — does the room go Vacant Dirty? Run night audit — does the business date advance? Check a report — does data appear in the Billing or Night Audit report?

Mistake Fix Created rooms before room types Create room types first, then rooms Created rates before room types exist Room types must exist before attaching rates Cashier cannot log in to register Link the user to a cashier in Cashiering Setup → Cashiers, and ensure the cash register exists Transaction code not appearing in folio Ensure the transaction code is active and not a payment-only code Night audit blocked by open shifts Close all cashier shifts before running night audit Business date is wrong Set the correct opening date in System Date before creating any reservations
Configuration Overview — Full configuration reference. Roles and Permissions — Setting up user access. Workflows — End-to-end operational flows once setup is complete.
Reminder Cycles define how often payment reminders are sent to AR accounts with outstanding balances.
Configuration → Account Receivables → Reminder Cycle
Field Description Name Name of the reminder cycle (e.g. Weekly, Monthly, 30-Day). Days Number of days between reminders. Active Whether this cycle is available for selection.

Reminder cycles are assigned to AR accounts (company or travel agent profiles). When an account has an outstanding balance, the system uses the assigned cycle to determine when to send the next reminder.
Account Receivables Setup Aging Levels

Cash Registers are the logical registers used for cashier login. Each cashier session is tied to a register. Before using any cashiering screen (Transaction, Cashier Shift, Quick Checkout), a user must log in to a cash register.
Configuration → General Configuration → Cash Register Setup

Field Description Short Code Register identifier (e.g. REG01, FRONT-DESK). Name Full name of the register (e.g. Front Desk Register 1). Sequence Display order. Hotel / Property The hotel this register belongs to (multi-property).

After creating a cash register, link it to cashier users in Configuration → Cashiering → Cashiers. A user must be linked to a cashier and that cashier must be linked to a register before they can log in.

When a user navigates to a cashiering screen, they are prompted to log in to a cash register if they have not already done so. The login screen shows available registers for their account. After logging in, the session is stored locally. The session is used for: Tracking which register posted each transaction (visible in Journal by Transaction) Shift drop / cashier close (the shift is tied to the register)

A user gets "No cash register session" when trying to close a shift. They must log in to a cash register first. Go to Cashiering → Transaction (or any cashiering screen) and complete Cash Register Login. Why doesn't a register appear in the login screen? The user may not be linked to a cashier that is linked to that register. Check Configuration → Cashiering → Cashiers.
Cashiering Setup → Cashiers Cashiering Overview Cashier Shift

Configure property details: Name, address, contact — Main property data Time zone — Used for dates and times Logo or branding — If used in folios or reports Access via Configuration → General Configuration → Hotel Information.
Service Charges define automatic charges that are added on top of eligible billing items. For example, a 10% service charge applied to all room charges, or a 5% tax on food & beverage.
Configuration → Cashiering → Service Charges

Field Description Short Code Abbreviated code (e.g. SVC, TAX10). Name Full name (e.g. Service Charge 10%, Government Tax). Sequence Display order. Percentage / Amount The charge rate (percentage) or fixed amount to apply. Transaction Code The transaction code used to post the service charge to the folio. Posting Rule Which charges this service charge applies to (e.g. room charges, F&B, all). Inclusive / Exclusive Whether the service charge is included in the posted amount (inclusive) or added on top (exclusive).

When a charge is posted to a folio using a transaction code that has a service charge rule, the system automatically calculates and posts the service charge as a separate line item using the linked transaction code. For example: Guest is charged 100 for room. A 10% service charge rule applies to room charges. The system automatically posts an additional 10 service charge line.
Transaction Codes Cashiering Setup Overview
Cashiers link user accounts to cash registers, enabling those users to log in to a cash register and use cashiering screens.
Configuration → Cashiering → Cashier

Field Description Short Code Cashier identifier (e.g. CSH01, JOHN). Name Cashier name (e.g. John Smith - Front Desk). Sequence Display order. User The user account linked to this cashier. Hotel / Property The hotel this cashier belongs to (multi-property).

Create a Cash Register in Cash Register Setup. Create a Cashier here and link it to a user account. The user can now log in to the cash register and use cashiering screens. When a cashier logs in, their session is recorded. All transactions posted during that session are attributed to their cashier/register in the Journal by Transaction and shift reports.

A user gets "not linked to a cashier" when trying to log in to a cash register. Create a Cashier record for that user here and link it to their user account. Can one user be linked to multiple cashiers? Typically one user maps to one cashier. If your property needs a user to work at multiple registers, check with your administrator.
Cash Register Setup Cashiering Overview Cashier Shift
Transaction Codes define every type of charge, payment, and adjustment that can be posted to a guest folio. Each code has a short code, name, group, and a set of flags that control how it behaves.
Configuration → Cashiering → Transaction Code

Field Description Short Code Abbreviated code used in reports and folios (e.g. ROOM, TAX, CASH). Name Full name of the transaction code (e.g. Room Charge, Service Tax, Cash Payment). Sequence Display order in lists and folios. Transaction Code Group The group this code belongs to — from Transaction Code Groups. Transaction Code Sub-Group Optional finer grouping — from Transaction Code Sub-Groups. Transaction Type Category: Others, Telephone Charges, Lodging, Minibar, Food & Beverage, Tax, Non Revenue. Default Currency The default currency for this code (if different from the property default). Inactive Mark the code as inactive so it no longer appears in posting screens.

These flags determine how the code behaves in payment scenarios: Flag Meaning Cash This code is used for cash payments. Credit Card This code is used for credit card payments. Bank Transfer This code is used for bank transfer payments. Mobile Payment This code is used for mobile payment methods. Other This code is used for other payment types. Manual This code can be posted manually by a cashier. Cashier Payment This code appears in the cashier payment screen. Deposit Payment This code is used for deposit payments. AR Payment This code is used for accounts receivable payments. Paid Out This code represents a paid-out (cash disbursement to a guest).

Flag Meaning Revenue Group This code contributes to revenue totals in reports. Generate Exclusive Tax is calculated on top of the posted amount (exclusive tax). Generate Inclusive The posted amount already includes tax; tax is extracted from it (inclusive tax). Check No Mandatory A reference/check number is required when posting this code.

Flag Meaning Internal Transaction Rate Code Only one code per hotel can have this flag. Used internally for rate posting. Internal Deposit Only one code per hotel can have this flag. Used as the GL target for deposit journal entries. Internal Deposit Code Links this code to the internal deposit GL code for journal posting.

Transaction codes are used in: Cashiering → Transaction — Post charges and payments to folios. Billing Report — Revenue and payment breakdown by transaction code. Night Audit — Revenue and payment summaries grouped by transaction code. Trial Balance — Debit/credit balance per transaction code. Routing Codes — Specify which transaction codes are routed to another payer. Service Charges — Link service charge rules to transaction codes.

Why can't I post a charge with a certain code? The code may be marked Inactive. Check the Inactive flag and reactivate if needed. Can I have two internal deposit codes? No — only one transaction code per hotel can have the Internal Deposit flag. The system will block saving a second one.
Transaction Code Groups Transaction Code Sub-Groups Service Charges Routing Codes Cashiering → Transaction
Add / edit articles — Items or services (e.g. minibar, laundry) used for POS-style charges. Access via Configuration → Cashiering → Article (or equivalent).

Services are additional offerings your property provides to guests — such as airport transfers, laundry, spa, or room service. Configuring services allows them to be selected and tracked in reservations and housekeeping.
Configuration → General Configuration → Services.

The list shows all configured services with their name, description, and active status. Column Description Name Service name (e.g. Airport Transfer, Laundry, Spa). Description Optional description of the service. Active Whether the service is currently available for selection.
Click Add or New Service. Fill in: Name — required. Short, clear name. Description — optional. Details about the service. Active — check to make it available. Save.
Click a service row to open it. Edit the fields and save.
Uncheck Active to hide the service from selection without deleting it. Existing records linked to the service are not affected.

Where are services used in the PMS? Services can be linked to reservations (as additional services requested by the guest) and to housekeeping tasks or track-it items depending on your configuration. Can I delete a service? Deleting a service that is linked to existing records may cause errors. Deactivate it instead by unchecking Active.
General Setup Overview — All general configuration items. Transportation Types — Configure transportation options for guest pickups. TrackIt — Track maintenance, lost & found, and service requests.

Departments are used for reporting or access control: Add / edit departments (e.g. Front Office, Housekeeping, F&B) Link to users or reports if configured Access via Configuration → General Configuration → Departments (or equivalent).
Cashiering setup includes transaction codes, transaction code groups/sub-groups, payment types, routing codes, cashiers, articles, exchange rates, and service charges.

Sub-menu Purpose Transaction Codes Add or edit transaction codes for posting charges. Transaction Code Groups High-level grouping (e.g. Room, F&B, Tax). Transaction Code Sub-Groups Finer grouping within a group. Payment Types Cash, Card, Bank Transfer, etc. Routing Codes Routing charges to company, travel agent, or other payers. Cashiers Users or registers that can log in to a cash register. Articles Items or services (e.g. minibar, laundry) for POS-style charges. Exchange Rates (Cashiering) Billing/payment conversion rates if cashiering uses separate rates. Service Charges Auto-added service charges (e.g. room, F&B). Access via Configuration → Cashiering (or equivalent sub-menus).
If the product uses a license or activation: View license status Enter or update license key (if allowed) Access via Configuration → General Configuration → License (or equivalent).
General setup covers hotel information, system date, users, user groups, departments, job titles, license, cash register setup, TrackIt, and transportation types.

Sub-menu Purpose Hotel Information Property details, address, contact, time zone, logo. System Date Business date used for reservations, rates, and reporting. Users User accounts, permissions, property assignment. User Groups Permission sets and group assignment. Departments Departments for reporting or access control. Job Titles Job titles for users or profiles. License License status and activation. Cash Register Setup Cash registers for cashier login. TrackIt Overview of the 4 Track It tables (Groups, Types, Actions, Locations) for Lost & Found. TrackIt — Groups Top-level item categories (Electronics, Clothing, Documents, etc.). TrackIt — Types Sub-categories within a group (Phone, Laptop, Passport, etc.). TrackIt — Actions Actions taken on items (Found, Returned, Disposed, etc.). TrackIt — Locations Where items were found or stored (Lobby, Room 101, HK Storage, etc.). Transportation Types Transportation types for pickup or guest transport. Services Additional services offered to guests (transfers, laundry, spa, etc.). Access via Configuration → General Configuration (or equivalent sub-menus).
If cashiering uses separate exchange rates: Add / edit rates for billing/payment conversion. Access via Configuration → Cashiering → Exchange Rate (or equivalent).
Track It is the item-tracking system used for Lost & Found in Housekeeping. It organises items using four reference tables — each has its own configuration page.
Configuration → General Configuration → Track It (sub-menus for each table)

Table Purpose Doc Groups Top-level categories (Electronics, Clothing, Documents, etc.) → Types Sub-categories within a group (Phone, Laptop, Passport, etc.) → Actions What happened to the item (Found, Returned, Disposed, etc.) → Locations Where the item was found or stored (Lobby, Room 101, HK Storage, etc.) →

When a housekeeper registers a found item in Housekeeping → Lost & Found, they select: Group → Type → Action → Location e.g. Electronics → Phone → Found → Room 101 All four dropdowns must have data configured here before Lost & Found can be used.

Set up in this order since Types, Actions, and Locations are independent of each other but all feed into the Lost & Found form: Groups — create top-level categories first Types — create sub-categories Actions — create actions with correct Category flag Locations — create locations

The Group/Type/Location dropdowns are empty in Lost & Found. The Track It reference data has not been set up. Add entries to each of the four tables. What is the Category flag on Track It Actions? It distinguishes between actions that represent a claim/resolution (e.g. Returned to Guest = Claimed = True) and actions that represent a found/held event (e.g. Found = False). Used for filtering and reporting in Lost & Found.
Housekeeping → Lost & Found — Using Track It in daily operations. General Setup Overview — All general configuration items.

The system (business) date is the "today" used for reservations, rates, and reporting when different from the real date. View / set the current business date. Night audit advances it to the next day when run. Access via Configuration → General Configuration → System Date Setup.

Job titles are used for users or profiles: Add / edit job titles (e.g. Receptionist, Manager) Link to users or profiles if configured Access via Configuration → General Configuration → Job Titles (or equivalent).
Transaction code sub-group — Finer grouping within a group. Used for reporting and folio layout. Access via Configuration → Cashiering (or equivalent sub-menus).
Track It Types are sub-categories within a Track It Group. They describe the specific kind of item (e.g. Phone within Electronics).
Configuration → General Configuration → Track It → Types
Field Required Description Short Code Yes Abbreviated code (e.g. PHONE, LAPTOP). Name Yes Type name (e.g. Phone, Laptop, Wallet, Passport). Sequence No Display order in dropdowns.

Group Types Electronics Phone, Laptop, Tablet, Charger, Camera, Earphones Clothing Shirt, Jacket, Shoes, Hat, Scarf Documents Passport, ID Card, Visa, Boarding Pass, Credit Card Accessories Watch, Bag, Umbrella, Glasses, Keys Jewellery Ring, Necklace, Bracelet, Earrings
Click Add. Enter a short code and name. Set sequence if needed. Save.

Track It — Groups — Top-level categories. Track It — Actions — What happened to the item. Track It — Locations — Where the item was found or stored. Housekeeping → Lost & Found — Using Track It in operations.
Users are the staff accounts that log in to the PMS. Each user has a username, password, assigned permissions (directly or via a user group), and optional profile details.
Configuration → General Configuration → Users

Field Description Username Login name (minimum 3 characters). Must be unique. Email User's email address. Password Set or reset the user's password (minimum 6 characters). First Name / Last Name Display name shown in the system. Job Title From Job Titles setup. Department From Departments setup. Used to filter staff in Housekeeping Tasks (Housekeeping dept) and Maintenance (Engineering dept). Profile Picture Optional photo shown in the user profile. User Groups Assign one or more user groups to inherit their permissions. Direct Permissions Assign specific permissions directly to the user (in addition to group permissions). Hotel / Property Assign the user to one or more hotels in multi-property setups. Active Whether the user can log in. Deactivate instead of deleting to preserve audit history.

The Department field is critical for two features: Housekeeping Tasks — Only users in the Housekeeping department appear in the Assign To dropdown when creating tasks. Maintenance — Only users in the Engineering (ENG) department appear in the Assigned To dropdown when creating maintenance records. Ensure the correct department short code is set: ENG for engineering staff.

Permissions control what each user can see and do. They are assigned via: User Groups — Assign a group; the user inherits all permissions in that group. Direct permissions — Add specific permissions on top of group permissions. See User Groups and Roles and Permissions for the full list of permission areas.

A user can't see a menu — what do I do? Check their user group and direct permissions. The required permission for each menu is listed in Roles and Permissions. Why doesn't a staff member appear in the Housekeeping Task Assign To dropdown? Their Department must be set to Housekeeping. Edit the user and assign the correct department. Why doesn't a staff member appear in the Maintenance Assigned To dropdown? Their Department must be set to a department with short code ENG (Engineering). Edit the user and assign the correct department.
User Groups Departments Job Titles Roles and Permissions
Account Types categorise AR (accounts receivable) accounts. Examples: Corporate, Travel Agent, Government, Group.
Configuration → Account Receivables → Account Type
Field Description Name The account type name (e.g. Corporate, Travel Agent). Description Optional description. Active Whether this type is available for selection.
Account types are assigned to company profiles and travel agent profiles when setting up their AR account. They are used for filtering and reporting in accounts receivable.
Account Receivables Setup Company Profiles Travel Agent Profiles

Add / edit out-of-order reasons (e.g. Repair, Renovation) Name, code (if used) Used when marking a room out of order in housekeeping or front desk. Access via Configuration → HouseKeeping & Maintenance → Out of Order/Service Reason (or equivalent).

User Groups define reusable permission sets. Assign a group to a user and they inherit all permissions in that group. This is the recommended way to manage permissions for multiple staff with the same role.
Configuration → General Configuration → User Groups

Field Description Short Code Abbreviated code (e.g. FD, CASH, HK, MGR). Name Group name (e.g. Front Desk, Cashier, Housekeeping, Manager). Sequence Display order. Permissions The list of permissions assigned to this group.

User Group (permissions) → assigned to → User → inherits all group permissions + any direct permissions added to the user A user can belong to one group. They inherit all permissions in that group. Direct permissions can be added on top of the group — useful for one-off exceptions (e.g. a front desk agent who also needs to run reports). If a permission is in the group, the user has it. If it is not in the group, the user does not have it — unless it is added directly to their account. Removing a permission from a group removes it from all users in that group immediately.
Permissions are organised by module. Each area has its own set of view / add / change / delete actions:

Permission What it allows view individualprofile See the individual profile list and detail add individualprofile Create new individual profiles change individualprofile Edit existing individual profiles delete individualprofile Delete individual profiles view companyprofile See company profiles add/change/delete companyprofile Manage company profiles view travelagentprofile See travel agent profiles add/change/delete travelagentprofile Manage travel agent profiles

Go to Configuration → General Configuration → User Groups. Click Add. Enter a Short Code and Name. Select the permissions to include — check each permission the group needs. Save. Go to Users and assign this group to the relevant user accounts.

Open the group from the list. Add or remove permissions as needed. Save. Changes apply immediately to all users in the group. warning Removing a permission from a group removes it from every user in that group. Check who is in the group before removing permissions.

A user can see a menu but cannot perform an action (e.g. can view but not edit). They have the view permission but not the add or change permission. Add the specific permission to their group or directly to their user account. Should I use groups or direct permissions? Use groups for roles shared by multiple staff. Use direct permissions for one-off exceptions on top of a group (e.g. a front desk agent who also needs to run night audit reports). A user is in a group but still cannot see a menu. Check that the group has the correct view permission for that module. Some menus require a specific permission name — see the tables above. Also confirm the user is saved with the correct group assigned. Can a user be in multiple groups? Each user is assigned one group. Use direct permissions to add extra access beyond the group.

Set general and hotel data first (hotel info, system date, users). Define profile and reservation reference data (countries, room types, rooms, reservation types, sources, etc.). Configure rates and packages. Configure cashiering (transaction codes, payment types, cashiers, service charges). Configure housekeeping (sections, tasks, reasons). Order may vary; some items depend on others (e.g. rooms need room types, rates may need rate categories).

Why don't I see Configuration at all? Your user may not have any setup permission. Access is per area (e.g. rooms, rates, users). Ask your administrator to grant the permissions you need (e.g. view/edit reservation setup, rate management, users). Where do I add a new room type or room? Go to Configuration → Reservation → Room Class / Room Types / Rooms. Define room classes and room types first, then add rooms. See Reservation Setup. Where do I add a new transaction code or payment type? Go to Configuration → Cashiering → Transaction Codes or Payment Types. See Cashiering Setup. What order should I set up configuration in? Start with General Configuration (hotel info, system date, users). Then Profile and Reservation reference data (countries, room types, rooms, reservation types). Then Rate Management, Cashiering, and HouseKeeping & Maintenance. Some items depend on others (e.g. rooms need room types). Can I change the system date manually? Usually only before night audit has been run for that date, and only if your role allows it. Go to Configuration → General Configuration → System Date Setup. Night audit advances the date; avoid changing it without following your property’s procedure.

Problem Cause Solution Configuration menu or category missing Your user lacks permission for that setup area. Ask your administrator to grant access to the relevant category (e.g. Reservation, Cashiering, General Configuration). Cannot save a new room / rate / code A required parent or field is missing (e.g. room type, rate category). Create the parent first (e.g. room type before rooms, rate category before rates). Check mandatory fields. Change does not appear in reservations or folios Some data is copied at reservation creation; or cache. New reservations use updated setup. Existing reservations may keep the value they had at creation. Refresh or reopen the screen if needed. User or cash register not in list User/cashier not created or not linked to the right property or register. In General Configurations, create or edit the user and assign permissions/cash register. Ensure the cash register exists in cash register setup. Lost & found reason or cancellation reason required but not in list Reason not yet created in setup. Add the reason in the relevant setup (e.g. Housekeeping → Lost & found reasons, Reservation Setup → Cancel reasons). For login, permissions, wrong property, and other general issues, see Troubleshooting.

Currencies defines the currencies your property accepts for billing and payments. The base currency is used for all internal calculations; other currencies are converted via exchange rates.
Configuration → Rate Management → Currency

Field Required Description Code Yes ISO 4217 currency code (e.g. USD, EUR, MMK, THB). Name Yes Full currency name (e.g. US Dollar, Euro, Myanmar Kyat). Symbol No Currency symbol (e.g. $, €, K). Decimal Places Yes Number of decimal places (e.g. 2 for USD, 0 for JPY). Is Base Currency No Mark one currency as the base (home) currency for the property. Active Yes Whether this currency is available for selection.

One currency should be marked as the base currency — this is the currency used for all internal accounting, reports, and folio balances. All other currencies are converted to the base currency using the exchange rates configured in Exchange Rates.

Can I have multiple base currencies? No — only one currency should be the base. All other currencies are foreign currencies converted at the exchange rate. A currency is not appearing in the payment modal. Ensure the currency is active and has an exchange rate configured in Exchange Rates.
Exchange Rates — Conversion rates between currencies. Cashiering → Exchange Rates — Cashiering-specific exchange rate setup. Rate Setup Overview

Packages bundle a room rate with additional inclusions (e.g. breakfast, airport transfer, spa credit). When a package rate is selected on a reservation, the included items are posted to the folio automatically.
Configuration → Rate Management → Package

Field Required Description Short Code Yes Abbreviated code (e.g. BB, HB, FB, ROM). Name Yes Package name (e.g. Bed & Breakfast, Half Board, Full Board, Romance Package). Description No Details of what is included. Transaction Codes Yes The transaction codes that make up the package (e.g. room charge + breakfast charge). Each code has an amount or percentage. Valid From / To No Date range the package is available. Active Yes Whether the package is available for selection.

When a reservation uses a package rate: The room charge is posted as normal. The package inclusions (e.g. breakfast) are posted as separate line items on the folio using the configured transaction codes. The package amount is split between the room and inclusion transaction codes for reporting purposes. This means revenue is correctly attributed — room revenue goes to the room transaction code, F&B revenue goes to the breakfast transaction code.

Short Code Name Inclusions RO Room Only Room charge only BB Bed & Breakfast Room + breakfast HB Half Board Room + breakfast + dinner FB Full Board Room + all meals AI All Inclusive Room + all meals + beverages

The package inclusions are not posting to the folio. Ensure the package transaction codes are active and the package is linked to the rate plan used on the reservation. Can a package have a different price per room type? Yes — packages are linked to rate plans, and rate plans are defined per room type. Set different amounts per room type in the rate setup.
Rates — Link packages to rate plans. Transaction Codes — The codes used for package inclusions. Rate Setup Overview

Exchange Rates define the conversion rate between foreign currencies and your base currency. They are used when guests pay in a foreign currency or when rates are quoted in a non-base currency.
Configuration → Rate Management → Exchange Rate

Field Required Description Currency Yes The foreign currency (e.g. USD, EUR, THB). Rate (MMK per unit) Yes How many units of base currency equal 1 unit of the foreign currency. Valid From Yes Start date for this rate. Valid To No End date. If blank, the rate is open-ended. Active Yes Whether this rate is currently in use.

When a payment is posted in a foreign currency, the system uses the exchange rate valid on the business date to convert the amount to the base currency for folio and reporting purposes. Example: If 1 USD = 2,100 MMK and a guest pays USD 100, the folio records MMK 210,000.
You can have multiple exchange rate records for the same currency with different validity periods. The system uses the rate whose Valid From date is on or before the transaction date.

The exchange rate is not applying in the payment modal. Ensure the currency is active in Currencies and the exchange rate has a valid date range covering today's business date. Should I update exchange rates daily? Update them whenever the rate changes significantly. For properties with high foreign currency volume, daily updates are recommended.

Reservation Types e.g. FIT, Group, Travel Agent. Reservation Methods e.g. Phone, Web, Walk-in. Booking Types e.g. Guaranteed, Non-guaranteed. Business Segments Market segment (e.g. Corporate, Leisure). Floor Plans Layout or floor grouping for room plan. Special Requests e.g. High floor, Late checkout. Room Status Statuses for room state (e.g. Available, Out of Order). Room Move Reasons for moving a guest to another room. Trace Codes Internal trace or follow-up. Origin Codes Origin of the reservation (e.g. region, campaign). Alert Codes Alerts or flags on the reservation. Discount Reasons Reason for a discount. Cancellation Reasons Reason for cancelling a reservation. Waitlist Reasons Reason or type for waitlist. Lost Reasons Why a reservation was lost. Refused Reasons Why a reservation was refused. Inactive Reasons Reasons for marking a profile, rate, or other entity inactive. Purpose of Stay Reason codes for why a guest is visiting (e.g. Business, Leisure, Conference). Access via Configuration → Reservation in the PMS. What you see depends on user permissions.
Rate setup covers rates, rate setup, rate classes, rate categories, markets, sources, packages, currencies, and exchange rates.

Sub-menu Purpose Rate Classes Broad grouping of rates (e.g. BAR, Corporate). Rate Categories Finer grouping (e.g. Weekday, Weekend). Rates Add or edit rates, room type, date range, amount. Markets Market segment or channel (e.g. Direct, OTA). Market Groups Top-level grouping of market codes (e.g. Corporate, Leisure). Sources Booking source and source group. Source Groups Top-level grouping of source codes (e.g. Online, Direct, GDS). Packages Packages with inclusions, validity, price or rate link. Currencies Supported currencies (code, symbol, decimals). Exchange Rates Rate to base currency, validity. Access via Configuration → Rate Management (or equivalent sub-menus).
Rooms are the individual sellable units in the property. Each room belongs to a room type and floor, and is linked to a hotel in multi-property setups.
Configuration → Reservation Setup → Rooms

Field Required Description Room Number Yes The room identifier shown on folios, room plan, and reports (e.g. 101, 201A). Room Type Yes The room type this room belongs to — from Room Types. Room Class No Auto-derived from the room type's class. Hotel Yes The hotel/property this room belongs to (multi-property). Floor No The floor — from Floor Plans. Used for grouping in Room Plan and Room Chart. Sequence No Display order within the floor/type. Description No Optional notes about the room. PM Room No If checked, this room is excluded from inventory counts (does not affect occupancy %). PM rooms can still have reservations, charges, and checkouts.

Each hotel has a total rooms limit set in Hotel Information. When you try to add a non-PM room that would exceed this limit, the system blocks the creation with an error. PM rooms do not count toward the limit.

Why can't I add more rooms? The hotel has reached its maximum room limit (set in Hotel Information → Total Rooms). Either increase the limit or mark the new room as a PM room. What is a PM room? A PM (Property Management) room is excluded from inventory and occupancy calculations. Use it for rooms that are managed but not sold as regular inventory (e.g. owner's suite, staff room). Why doesn't a room appear in the Room Plan or Room Chart? Ensure the room has a Floor assigned. Rooms without a floor may not appear in floor-grouped views.
Room Types Room Classes Floor Plans Front Desk → Room Plan Housekeeping → Room Chart
Visa Types are the categories of visas that guests may hold — for example, Tourist, Business, Transit, Diplomatic. They are stored on individual guest profiles for immigration and reporting purposes.
Configuration → Profile Setup → Visa Type
Field Description Name The visa type name (e.g. Tourist Visa, Business Visa, Transit Visa). Short Code Abbreviated code. Active Whether this type is available for selection on guest profiles.
Visa type is recorded on an individual guest profile along with visa number, issue date, and expiry date. This data appears in the Guest Information Report for immigration compliance.
Profile Setup Overview Individual Profiles Guest Information Report
Reservation Types classify bookings and control which fields are required when creating a reservation. Examples: Regular, Walk-In, Group, Complimentary, House Use.
Configuration → Reservation Setup → Reservation Type

Field Required Description Short Code Yes Abbreviated code (e.g. REG, WI, GRP). Walk-In types should use WI or WALK for the Room Chart walk-in feature to work. Name Yes Full name (e.g. Regular, Walk-In, Group). Sequence No Display order in dropdowns. Inactive No Mark as inactive to hide from reservation forms.

These flags control what is required when a reservation of this type is created: Flag Effect Arrival Time Required The ETA (Estimated Time of Arrival) field becomes mandatory on the reservation form. Credit Card Required The credit card number field becomes mandatory. Deposit Required The deposit amount field becomes mandatory. Phone Required The guest phone number field becomes mandatory. Detach Inventory When checked, reservations of this type do not consume room inventory (e.g. House Use, Complimentary).

For the Room Chart walk-in feature to work, create a reservation type with short code WI or WALK. When a user clicks Walk-In on a vacant room in the Room Chart, the new reservation form opens with this type pre-selected.

Why is the deposit field greyed out on a reservation? The selected reservation type does not have Deposit Required enabled. Enable it on the reservation type if deposits should be collected for that type. Why does the Room Chart not show a Walk-In button? No reservation type with short code WI or WALK exists. Create one in this screen.
Reservations → List — Reservation form uses these types. Housekeeping → Room Chart — Walk-In uses the WI/WALK type. Reservation Setup Overview
Business segment — Market segment (e.g. Corporate, Leisure). Used in reservations and reporting. Access via Configuration → Reservation → Business Segments.
Trace code — Internal trace or follow-up. Used in reservations and reporting. Access via Configuration → Reservation → Trace Codes.
Room status — Statuses used for room state (e.g. Available, Out of Order). May overlap with housekeeping status. Access via Configuration → Reservation → Room Status.
Reservation refused reason — Why a reservation was refused. Used when recording refused reservations. Access via Configuration → Reservation → Reservation Refused Reasons.
This page covers profile details (viewing and editing your user profile) and changing your password.

Your user profile stores your display name, job title, contact details, and similar information. Access it from the profile or account area in the top bar (e.g. your name or avatar → Profile / Profile details). From the profile details screen you can: View your username, email, job title, and other stored fields Edit allowed fields (depending on configuration) Go to Change password if the option is shown

You can change your password from Profile → Change password (or the link in the profile/account menu). The change-password screen is also available at Change Password in the app when your administrator has exposed it.

Open Change password (from your profile/account menu or direct link). Enter your current password. Enter your new password. It must meet the following rules: At least 6 characters At least one uppercase letter At least one number At least one special character (e.g. ! @ # $ %) Enter the new password again in the confirmation field. Click Change password (or Submit). If the change succeeds, you are signed out and redirected to the login page. Log in again using your new password. :::tip Forgot current password? You cannot change your password without the current one. If you have forgotten it, contact your administrator or IT support to reset your password. :::
You must use the new password for all future logins. Other sessions may be invalidated; you may need to log in again on other devices or tabs.
Room class — Broad category (e.g. Standard, Deluxe). Define these first; rooms and room types often reference them. Access via Configuration → Reservation → Room Class.
