Why is Post Room Charge greyed out? You must be logged in to a cash register. The reservation must be In-House. You also need the post_room_charge permission — ask your administrator to grant it. Night audit already ran but room charges are missing for some reservations. This can happen if the reservation had no room or rate assigned at audit time. Assign the room and rate, then use Post Room Charge to post the missing charges. Can I post room charges for a checked-out reservation? Post Room Charge is typically available for In-House reservations. For checked-out reservations with missing charges, contact your administrator — corrections may need to be made via a manual adjustment. The rate shown is wrong. The rate comes from the reservation's daily rate setup. If the rate is incorrect, edit the reservation's rate first, then post the room charge.

Cashiering → Transaction — Full folio management; Post Room Charge is available here. Billing (Guest Folio) — Folio management with Post Room Charge. Night Audit — Automatic room charge posting during night audit. Configuration → Cashiering → Transaction Codes — Room charge transaction code setup.

The Trial Balance Report aggregates the night audit guest ledger by transaction code for a selected audit run, showing total debits, total credits, and the net balance per code. :::tip Having a problem? See the main Troubleshooting page for general issues, or the Reports Overview for how to run and export reports. :::
From the sidebar, click Report → Trial Balance.

Filter Description Start Date Optional date range start to narrow the audit run list. End Date Optional date range end. Audit Run Select a specific night audit run to view its trial balance. You must select an Audit Run to generate the report. The audit run dropdown lists completed runs with their date and status.

Each row is one transaction code that appeared in the selected audit run's guest ledger. Column Description Transaction Code Short code and name of the transaction code. Total Debit Sum of all debit entries for that code in the audit run. Total Credit Sum of all credit entries for that code in the audit run. Balance Debit minus Credit for that code. The report footer shows grand totals: Total Debit, Total Credit, and overall Balance.

The trial balance is built from the Night Audit Guest Ledger — the record of all charges and payments posted during the night audit run. Each ledger entry is linked to a transaction code, and this report groups and sums them. A balanced trial balance (total debit = total credit) confirms that all postings for that audit run are in balance.
Use the export button (if available) to download as Excel or PDF for accounting reconciliation.

Why is the report empty? You must select an audit run from the dropdown. If no audit runs appear, night audit has not been run yet, or you may not have permission to view night audit data. What does a non-zero balance mean? A non-zero overall balance may indicate unposted items, voided transactions, or an incomplete audit run. Review the night audit run and exceptions.

Night Audit — Run night audit and view audit runs. Night Audit Reports — Daily summary, revenue, and guest ledger for an audit run. Billing Reports — Revenue and payment detail by date range. Reports Overview — How to run and export reports.

The Registration Form (also called the registration card) is a printable guest check-in document. It contains the guest's personal details, stay information, and a signature line for the guest to sign at check-in. :::tip Having a problem? See Reservations Overview for common FAQs and troubleshooting. :::
From a reservation detail page, click Registration Form (or the registration form button in the reservation actions).

Section Content Property Header Hotel name, address, logo. Guest Details Full name, nationality, date of birth, passport/NRC number, phone, email. Stay Details Arrival date, departure date, number of nights, room number, room type. Rate Rate plan and nightly rate. Additional Guests Names of additional guests on the reservation (if any). Special Requests Any special requests noted on the reservation. Signature Line Space for the guest to sign and date. Terms Any check-in terms or policies configured for the property.

Open the reservation (from the reservation list or detail page). Click Registration Form. A modal opens with a formatted preview of the registration card. Click Print to print. Print the form before the guest arrives so it is ready at the front desk.

At check-in — present to the guest for review and signature. For immigration compliance — the signed registration card is often required by local regulations as proof of guest identity and stay. For group check-ins — print one form per room/reservation.

For the registration form to be complete, the guest profile should have: Full name (first and last) Nationality Date of birth Passport or NRC number These fields are stored on the Individual Profile. If they are missing, fill them in on the profile before printing.

The registration form is missing the guest's passport number. Passport details are stored on the individual profile under Step 3 — Travel Documents. Open the profile in Profile → Individual Profile and fill in the passport fields. Can I print registration forms for all arrivals at once? Currently, registration forms are generated per reservation. Open each reservation and print individually. The hotel name or address is wrong on the form. Update the property details in Configuration → General Configuration → Hotel Information.

Reservations Overview — Full reservation management. Confirmation Letter — Booking confirmation document. Individual Profile — Guest profile with travel document fields. Configuration → Hotel Information — Property details shown on the form.
The Departures view shows all in-house reservations with a departure date on the selected date. Use it to prepare folios, process check-outs, and hand rooms back to housekeeping.
From the sidebar: Reservation → Departures tab (or filter the reservation list by departure date).

Reservations with departure date matching the selected date, showing: Reservation ID and guest name Room number and room type Arrival and departure dates, nights Reservation status (In-House) ETD (estimated departure time) if set Folio balance or link to billing

Before checking out a guest: Open the reservation and review the folio in Cashiering → Transaction. Post any outstanding charges (e.g. last night's room charge, minibar, late checkout fee). Confirm the folio balance is zero or collect the remaining payment. If the guest is paying by company or travel agent routing, confirm the routing is set up correctly.

From the departures list or the reservation detail: Open the reservation. Ensure the folio balance is settled (zero or routed to a payer). Click Check Out. The status changes to Checked Out. The reservation moves to Front Desk → Checked-Out. The room status updates to Vacant Dirty — housekeeping will clean it.
For multiple departures, use Cashiering → Quick Checkout to process several reservations at once without opening each folio individually.
If a guest stays past the standard checkout time, post a late checkout charge from Cashiering → Transaction before checking out.

All Reservations — Full reservation list. Arrivals — Guests checking in today. Front Desk → Checked-Out — Departed guests list. Cashiering → Transaction — Settle folio before checkout. Cashiering → Quick Checkout — Batch checkout for multiple departures. Housekeeping → Room Status — Room becomes Vacant Dirty after checkout.
This guide walks through the full checkout process — from verifying the folio to checking out the guest and updating room status.
Verify folio → Post missing charges → Take final payment → Checkout → Room goes Vacant Dirty

Go to one of: Cashiering → Transaction — select the reservation from the in-house list. Billing — open from the reservation detail or Front Desk → In-House. Front Desk → In-House — click the folio/billing action on the guest row. Cashiering → Quick Checkout — for batch processing of multiple departures.

Check the transaction table for the selected folio: Confirm all room charges are posted (one per night of stay). Check for any outstanding incidentals (minibar, F&B, laundry, etc.). If room charges are missing, use Post Room Charge to post them manually.

If a charge is missing: Click Charge (or Add Item). Select the transaction code. Enter quantity and unit price. Save. The charge posts to the folio. For missing room charges specifically, use Post Room Charge and select the date.

Click Payment. Select the payment type (cash, card, bank transfer, etc.). Enter the amount equal to the folio balance. Save. The balance should reach zero. If the guest is paying in a foreign currency, the exchange rate is applied automatically. If there is an overpayment (negative balance), add a negative payment (refund) to bring it to zero.

Once all folio balances are zero: Click Checkout in the billing header or reservation detail. Confirm. The reservation status changes to Checked Out. The room status automatically changes to Vacant Dirty. If using Quick Checkout, checkout triggers automatically when the balance reaches zero.
After checkout, print or email the final folio to the guest: Click Print Folio. Select the folio number. Print or save as PDF.

After checkout, the room is automatically set to Vacant Dirty. The housekeeping team: Sees the room in Housekeeping → Room Status as Vacant Dirty. Cleans the room. Updates the status to Vacant Clean (or Inspected). The room is now available for the next guest.

The Checkout button is greyed out. The folio balance must be zero before checkout. Check all folios — a reservation can have multiple folios, each needing a zero balance. Room charges are missing for some nights. Use Post Room Charge in the billing/transaction screen to post charges for specific dates. Or check if night audit was run for those dates. The guest wants to pay with multiple payment types. Post multiple payments (e.g. part cash, part card) until the total equals the balance. The guest disputes a charge. Void the disputed charge (with a void reason), or adjust the amount. Then take payment for the corrected balance.

Billing (Guest Folio) — Full folio management. Cashiering → Transaction — Cashier workspace. Cashiering → Quick Checkout — Batch checkout for multiple departures. Housekeeping → Room Status — Update room to Vacant Clean after checkout. Night Audit — Post room charges and close the business date.

The Reservation module is used to create, view, edit, and manage guest reservations. It connects to guest profiles, rooms, rates, billing, and housekeeping. :::tip Having a problem? This page has Common FAQs and Troubleshooting below. For issues in other modules, see the main Troubleshooting page. :::

View Purpose All Reservations Full list — search, filter, create, edit, check in/out. Arrivals Guests arriving on a selected date. Departures Guests departing on a selected date. Waitlist Requests held when no room or rate is available. Cancellations Cancelled reservations with reasons. Confirmation Letter Printable booking confirmation for the guest. Registration Form Guest registration card for check-in signature.

Status Meaning Reserved / Confirmed Booking is held; guest not yet arrived. In-House Guest has checked in; charges post to folio. Checked Out Stay is complete; balance should be settled. Cancelled Booking is no longer active. Waitlist Request held pending availability. No Show Guest did not arrive on the arrival date.

Create a reservation (from the list, Room Plan, or Room Chart). Assign a room when available. Check in the guest on arrival — status moves to In-House. Charges post to the folio during the stay. Check out the guest on departure — settle the folio balance. Night audit closes the business date and posts room charges. Check-in and check-out can be done from the reservation detail, Front Desk → In-House, or Front Desk → Room Plan.

Why don't I see the Reservation menu? Your user may not have reservation permission. Ask your administrator to grant access. How do I check in a guest? Open the reservation, ensure the room is Vacant Clean in Housekeeping, then use the Check In action. The guest appears in Front Desk → In-House. How do I add additional guests to a reservation? On the reservation form, Step 1 (Guest Info), click Add Another Guest. Each additional guest can be linked to an existing individual profile or entered manually. Where is the waitlist? The Waitlist tab appears on the Reservation screen. Use it when no suitable room or rate is available; convert to a full reservation when space opens. Can I cancel and recreate instead of editing dates? Prefer editing the reservation when only dates or room change. Use cancellation when the stay is truly cancelled — cancellation creates a record with a reason and cannot be undone.

Problem Cause Solution Room type not available Dates fully booked, room out of order, or rate not set for those dates. Check room status in Housekeeping. Confirm rate/availability in Configuration for that room type and date. Cannot check in Reservation not in a check-in-allowed status, or room not ready. Ensure status is Reserved/Confirmed. Set the room to Vacant Clean in Housekeeping before check-in. Cannot change room Target room occupied or not sellable; missing room-move permission or reason. Verify target room is available. Check permissions and that a room-move reason exists in Configuration. Guest or payer not in list Profile may not exist or wrong type searched. Search in the correct profile type (individual/company/travel agent), or create a new profile. Cancellation reason required Configuration requires a reason when cancelling. Select a cancellation reason from the list; add new reasons in Configuration → Reservation Setup. Deposit amount required Reservation type is configured to require a deposit. Enter a deposit amount in the Booking Info step. Phone number required Reservation type is configured to require a phone number. Enter the guest's phone number in the Guest Info step. Credit card required Reservation type or payment type requires a credit card. Enter the credit card number in the Booking Info step.

Profiles — Individual, company, and travel agent profiles. Front Desk — Room plan, in-house list, checked-out list. Cashiering — Post charges, take payments, view folios. Housekeeping — Room status before check-in. Night Audit — Close the business date and post room charges. Configuration — Reservation types, sources, room types, rates, cancellation reasons.
The Waitlist holds reservation requests when no suitable room or rate is available. When space opens, convert the entry to a full reservation.
From the sidebar: Reservation → Waitlist tab.
Use the waitlist when: The requested room type is fully booked for the dates. The requested rate is closed or unavailable. You want to hold the guest's interest without confirming a room.

Go to Reservation → Waitlist. Click Add or New Waitlist. Fill in the requested dates, room type, guest profile, and contact details. Select a Waitlist Reason (from Configuration → Reservation Setup → Waitlist Reasons). Save. The entry is held with status Waitlist.

When a room or rate becomes available: Open the waitlist entry. Click Convert (or equivalent action). Assign a room and rate. The entry becomes a full reservation with status Reserved/Confirmed. Notify the guest.
The waitlist shows all pending entries with guest name, requested dates, room type, reason, and date added. Filter by date range or status to manage the queue.
All Reservations — Full reservation list; converted waitlist entries appear here. Reservations Overview — Statuses and workflow. Configuration → Waitlist Reasons — Set up waitlist reason codes.
The Arrivals view shows all reservations with an arrival date on the selected date. Use it to prepare for check-ins, confirm room readiness, and process arrivals efficiently.
From the sidebar: Reservation → Arrivals tab (or filter the reservation list by arrival date).

Reservations with arrival date matching the selected date, showing: Reservation ID and guest name Room type and room number (if assigned) Arrival and departure dates, nights Reservation status (e.g. Reserved, Confirmed) Rate amount ETA (estimated arrival time) if set Special requests and alerts

Before checking in a guest: Confirm the room is assigned — if no room number is set, assign one from the reservation or Room Plan. Check Housekeeping to ensure the room is Vacant Clean (VC). A room that is Vacant Dirty cannot be checked in until cleaned. Review any alerts or special requests on the reservation. Settle any pre-arrival deposit if required by the reservation type.

From the arrivals list or the reservation detail: Open the reservation. Confirm all required fields are filled (room, rate, guest details). Click Check In. The status changes to In-House. The guest appears in Front Desk → In-House. If the room is not ready, the check-in action may be blocked. Update the room status in Housekeeping first.

For guests without a prior reservation, use Housekeeping → Room Chart to select a Vacant Clean room and click Walk-In. This opens the new reservation form pre-filled with the Walk-In reservation type, room, and today's arrival date.

All Reservations — Full reservation list and creation. Departures — Guests checking out today. Front Desk → Room Plan — Assign rooms and check in from the calendar view. Housekeeping → Room Chart — Visual room status grid; walk-in from vacant rooms. Housekeeping → Room Status — Update room status before check-in.

The Report module provides predefined reports for reservations, front desk, billing (including folio summary), housekeeping, and night audit. Use it to run folio, cash register, and night audit reports from one place. In the app, Report is a menu with categories you can choose. :::tip Having a problem? See the main Troubleshooting page — it links to module-specific FAQs and troubleshooting for Reservations, Front Desk, Cashiering, Housekeeping, Night Audit, Dashboard, Configuration, and Profiles. ::: Category Purpose Reservation Arrivals/departures, in-house, pickup, cancellations, revenue or occupancy by segment/source/rate. Frontdesk Room occupancy, room status summary, room plan or availability. Billing Revenue by transaction code or category, payments by type, outstanding balances, folio summary. Housekeeping Room status, task completion, lost & found log, maintenance log, out-of-order list. Night Audit Daily summary, revenue summary, room revenue, exceptions, guest ledger for the audit date. Guest Information In-house guest list with full profile details (passport, visa, nationality, VIP level) for a business date. Trial Balance Debit/credit balance per transaction code for a night audit run. Manager Report Consolidated daily summary: occupancy, revenue, payments, outstanding, and breakdowns by transaction code, payment type, and room type.

From the sidebar, click Report. Choose a category (Reservation, Frontdesk, Billing, Housekeeping, Night Audit) from the menu or from a dropdown/tabs on the report page. What you see depends on user permissions.

Select category — Reservation, Frontdesk, Billing, Housekeeping, or Night Audit. Set filters — Date range, property, department, status, etc. Run / Apply — Generate the report. View — Results appear in a table or summary. Export — If available, export to Excel, PDF, or CSV for accounting or management.

Night Audit — Run night audit; reports from each run and Night Audit reports (daily summary, guest ledger, folio data). Cashiering — Transaction, Cashier Shift, folio and cash register data; Billing reports for revenue and outstanding balances. Reservations — For reservation and occupancy data. Housekeeping — For room status and tasks.

Individual Profile stores personal data for guests and contacts used in reservations. It is the most detailed profile type, with three sections: Personal Info, Address, and Travel Documents. :::tip Having a problem? See Profiles Overview for common FAQs and troubleshooting. :::
From the sidebar: Profile → Individual Profile.

The list shows all individual profiles with columns for name, email, phone, nationality, VIP level, and profile display ID. Use the search bar to find by name, email, or phone. Filters narrow by VIP level, nationality, or other criteria.
Click Add or New Individual Profile. The form is split into three steps:

Field Required Description Last Name Yes Guest's last name. First Name Yes Guest's first name. Middle Name No Middle name. Title No Salutation (Mr, Mrs, Ms, Dr, etc.) — from Titles setup. Email Yes Contact email. Phone Yes Contact phone number. Date of Birth No Used for guest age and immigration reports. NRC No National Registration Card number. Gender No Male / Female / Other. Father Name No Father's name (used for immigration). Occupation No Guest's occupation. Nationality No From Nationalities setup. Language No Preferred language — from Languages setup. VIP Status No VIP level — from VIP Levels setup. Note No Internal notes about the guest.

Address fields cascade: select Region → Country → State/Division → City → Postal Code. Each level filters the options for the next. Field Description Address Free-text street address. Region From Regions setup. Country From Countries setup, filtered by region. State/Division/Region From States setup, filtered by country. City From Cities setup, filtered by state. Postal Code From Postal Codes setup, filtered by city.

Field Description Passport Number Guest's passport number. Passport Issue Date Date passport was issued. Passport Expiry Date Date passport expires. Visa Type From Visa Types setup. Visa Number Visa document number. Visa Issue Date Date visa was issued. Visa Expiry Date Date visa expires. Arrival to Country Date Date the guest arrived in the country. Travel document data appears in the Guest Information Report for immigration compliance.
Open a profile from the list (click the row or name). The detail page shows all three sections. Click Edit to modify fields, then Save.

When creating or editing a reservation, search for an individual profile to assign as: Primary Guest — the person staying in the room Additional Guest — extra guests linked to the same reservation Both primary and additional guests appear in the Guest Information Report.
The ar_number field links the guest to an accounts receivable account. This is managed by the accounts team and used for direct billing.

Why are some fields greyed out or not visible? The list form (quick create) shows fewer fields than the detail page. Open the profile after saving to fill in all sections including travel documents. Can I search by passport number? Use the search bar on the list page. Search covers name, email, and phone. For passport-specific lookup, use the Guest Information Report filtered by business date. The address dropdowns are empty. Address options depend on setup. Region must be selected before Country appears; Country before State; State before City; City before Postal Code. If a level is empty, the parent setup data may be missing — check Configuration → Profile Setup.

Profiles Overview Guest Information Report — In-house guest list with full profile and travel document data. Reservations — Assign individual profiles as guests. Configuration → Profile Setup — Titles, nationalities, VIP levels, languages, visa types, address data.

This page describes two common end-to-end workflows: check-in (from reservation to in-house) and end-of-day cashier and night audit. Use them as reference when training staff or writing local procedures.
From a confirmed reservation to an in-house guest with a ready room and open folio.
Guest has a confirmed (or equivalent) reservation for today’s arrival. Reservation shows correct guest, room type (or room), dates, and rate. See Reservations to create or edit reservations.
In Housekeeping, ensure the assigned (or to-be-assigned) room is Vacant Clean or Ready. If the room is dirty or under maintenance, change status or assign another room. See Housekeeping.

In Reservation or Front Desk → Room plan, assign a specific room number if the reservation only had a room type. Or use Room plan to see which room is allocated and that it is available. See Front Desk.

Open the reservation (from Reservation list or Front Desk → Room plan or In-house). Use Check in (or equivalent). The reservation status becomes In-house (or Checked in). The guest is now in-house and room charges can post to the folio.

Go to Cashiering → Transaction (after cash register login if required). Select the reservation. The folio is used for all charges and payments during the stay. Post room and tax as per your property (e.g. at check-in or via night audit). Add any deposit or advance payment if required. See Cashiering.
Reservation (confirmed) → Room ready (Housekeeping) → Assign room → Check in → Folio open for charges/payments
Closing the business day: cashier reconciliation, then night audit.
documents.s: End-of-Day Cashier and Night Audit Flow

documents.t: Finish posting all charges and payments for the day. In Cashiering → Cashier shift, close the shift (or equivalent): Declare expected vs actual cash (and other payment types if required). Resolve any variance per your property’s policy. Ensure all folios that should be settled for that day are handled. See Cashiering.

In Night Audit, open the last run or prepare a new run. Review exceptions (e.g. open balances, high balance, missing data). Resolve critical exceptions before running the final audit (e.g. post missing room charges, correct rates, or document variances). See Night Audit.

In Night Audit, start Run night audit (or equivalent). Confirm the business date to close (usually today). Start the run. The system will: Post room charges for all in-house guests for that night. Advance the system (business) date to the next day (if configured). Generate audit reports and update statistics. See Night Audit.
Open the completed audit run. Check Overview, Revenue, Payments, Room status, and Exceptions. Use Reports or Guest ledger for that date if you need to file or reconcile with accounting. See Reports.
Per your property’s policy, back up the database or export key reports (e.g. night audit summary, revenue, guest ledger) for the closed date.
All charges/payments done → Close cashier shift → Fix critical exceptions → Run night audit → Review reports / backup

Reservations — Creating and managing reservations Front Desk — Room plan, in-house, checked-out Cashiering — Transactions, shift, quick checkout Housekeeping — Room status and tasks Night Audit — Running audit and reviewing runs Reports — Running and exporting reports
Guidelines for keeping the Hotelium PMS manual buildable, link-valid, and consistent.

Use absolute paths for doc-to-doc links so they work from any page and from the built site: Do: [Introduction](/docs/intro), [Reservations](/docs/operations/reservations/overview) Avoid: [Introduction](../intro), [Reservations](./reservations) (relative paths) Internal anchors are fine: [Section](#section-name).

Build = link check: npm run build fails if any doc link is broken (onBrokenLinks: 'throw' in docusaurus.config.js). CI: If GitHub Actions is used, the Docs workflow runs on push/PR to main (or master) and runs npm ci and npm run build. Fix broken links before merging. Local: Run npm run build (or npm run validate) before pushing to catch broken links early.

When updating procedures, aim to add or refresh these screenshots. See Screenshot guide for filenames and where to use them. Priority Screenshot Doc(s) Filename Must Login screen Intro login.png Must Dashboard Dashboard dashboard.png Must Reservation list or new-reservation form Reservations reservation-list.png or reservation-form.png Must Transaction / folio Cashiering folio.png Must Night audit (runs or run dialog) Night Audit night-audit.png Nice Main sidebar Intro sidebar.png Nice Room plan Front Desk room-plan.png Nice Housekeeping management Housekeeping housekeeping.png Nice Notifications bell Notifications notifications-bell.png Nice Change password Profile and Security change-password.png Nice System Settings / config Configuration overview configuration.png Save images under static/img/docs/ and reference as /img/docs/<filename>.png.

If you need to ship multiple manual versions (e.g. v1.0, v1.1), use Docusaurus versioning: Add a version, e.g. docusaurus docs:version 1.0. Docs are versioned under /docs/1.0/...; current is /docs/.... Document your release process (when to cut a version, how to update the dropdown).
Command Purpose npm ci Clean install (use in CI). npm run build Build site; fails on broken links. npm run validate Same as npm run build (alias for CI or pre-commit). npm run start Local dev server.

docs/ — Markdown source; sidebar is autogenerated from here. Use sidebar_position in frontmatter and _category_.json for order. static/ — Served at /. Put screenshots in static/img/docs/, scripts in static/js/. src/ — React components, CSS, theme overrides. docusaurus.config.js — Title, theme, navbar, footer, plugins (search, etc.). sidebars.js — Sidebar config (currently autogenerated from docs/).
Screenshot guide — Full list of recommended screenshots and how to add them.
The Cancellations view lists all cancelled reservations. Use it to review cancellation history, filter by reason or date, and manage cancellation records.
From the sidebar: Reservation → Cancellations tab.
Cancelled reservations with: Reservation ID and guest name Original arrival and departure dates Cancellation date Cancellation reason Room type and rate

To cancel a reservation: Open the reservation from the All Reservations list. Click Cancel from the reservation actions. A modal appears — select a Cancellation Reason from the dropdown. Confirm. The status changes to Cancelled and the reservation moves to the Cancellations list. Cancellation reasons are configured in Configuration → Reservation Setup → Cancellation Reasons. caution Cancellation cannot be undone. If you need to reinstate a cancelled reservation, create a new one.

Cancellation reasons are required when cancelling. Common examples: Guest Request No Show Duplicate Booking Rate Not Available Property Request Add or manage reasons in Configuration → Reservation Setup → Cancellation Reasons.
Use filters to narrow the list by: Date range (cancellation date or original arrival date) Cancellation reason Guest name or reservation ID Room type
All Reservations — Cancel from the reservation detail. Reservations Overview — Statuses and workflow. Configuration → Cancellation Reasons — Set up cancellation reason codes.
The All Reservations list shows every booking. Use it to search, filter, create, edit, and manage reservations.
From the sidebar: Reservation. The main list opens by default.

Typical columns include: Reservation ID, Guest Name, Room Type, Room Number, Arrival Date, Departure Date, Nights, Status, Rate Amount, Reservation Type, Source, Market. Use the search bar and filters to narrow results by guest name, confirmation number, dates, status, room type, source, market, and more.
Click New Reservation. The form has three steps:

Search for an existing individual profile or enter guest details manually. Field Required Description Guest Search — Search existing individual profiles by name. Selecting a profile auto-fills the fields below. Title No Salutation (Mr, Mrs, Ms, Dr, etc.). First Name No Guest's first name. Middle Name No Guest's middle name. Last Name Yes Guest's last name. Email No Guest's email. Phone Conditional Required if the selected reservation type has Phone Required enabled. NRC No National Registration Card number. Nationality No From Nationalities setup. Country No From Countries setup. VIP Status No From VIP Levels setup. Additional Guests — Click Add Another Guest to add more guests to the same reservation. Each additional guest can be linked to an existing profile or entered manually. Use the action menu on each guest row to Make Primary Guest (swap with the primary) or Remove Guest. Travel Agent / Company — Below the guest fields, assign a Travel Agent profile and/or Company Profile as payer or organiser.

Two sections: Stay Information and Geographical Data. Stay Information: Field Required Description Arrival Date Yes Check-in date. Defaults to the current business date. Cannot be before the business date. Number of Nights No Auto-calculates departure date. Departure Date Yes Check-out date. Auto-calculated from arrival + nights. Room Type No Select the room type. Filters available rooms and rate codes. Rate Code No Rate plan for the stay. Options depend on the selected room type and arrival date. Room Number No Specific room. Disabled when Number of Rooms > 1. Number of Rooms No For multi-room bookings. When > 1, room number is not assigned individually. No. of Adults No Number of adults. Affects rate calculation if rate has per-adult pricing. Rate Amount No Nightly rate. Auto-filled from rate code selection. Total Amount No Calculated: rate × nights. Daily Rate No Click View Daily Rate to set a custom rate per night (overrides the flat rate). Package No Package selection (currently read-only; set via rate code). Reservation Type Yes Type of reservation (e.g. Regular, Walk-In, Group). Controls required fields (phone, credit card, deposit, arrival time). Deposit Amount Conditional Required if reservation type has Deposit Required enabled. Payment Type No How the guest will pay (cash, card, etc.). May trigger credit card requirement. Credit Card Number Conditional Required if reservation type or payment type requires it. Masked by default. ETA (Estimated Arrival Time) Conditional Required if reservation type has Arrival Time Required enabled. ETD (Estimated Departure Time) No Expected departure time. Geographical Data: Field Description Market Market segment for the booking — from Markets setup. Source Booking source (e.g. Direct, OTA, Travel Agent) — from Sources setup. Purpose of Stay Reason for the visit — from Purpose of Stay setup. Transportation — Click Add Transportation Info to expand the transportation step (optional).

Only shown if you clicked Add Transportation Info in Step 2. Field Description Pick Up Required Yes/No toggle. Enables pickup detail fields. Pickup Location Where to pick up the guest. Pick Up Time Time of pickup. Pickup Transportation Type Vehicle/transport type — from Transportation Types setup. Pickup Transport Number Flight number, train number, etc. Drop Off Required Yes/No toggle. Enables drop-off detail fields. Drop-off Location Where to drop off the guest. Drop Off Time Time of drop-off. Dropoff Transportation Type Vehicle/transport type. Dropoff Transport Number Flight number, train number, etc.

Open a reservation from the list (click the row). The detail page shows the same three-step form in view mode. Click Edit to make changes. Note for in-house reservations: Arrival date and room type are locked while the guest is checked in. To move the guest to another room, use Room Move from the In-House list. Nights, departure date, and rate amount remain editable.

On the Booking Info step of an existing reservation, an Activity card shows: Button Description Alerts View and manage reservation alerts (shown as warning banners at the top of the reservation). Comments Internal comments/notes on the reservation. Messages Guest-facing messages linked to the reservation. Each button shows a count badge when items exist.

From the reservation detail header, available actions depend on the current status and your permissions: Action When Available Description Check In Status: Reserved/Confirmed Moves the reservation to In-House. Room must be Vacant Clean. Check Out Status: In-House Moves the reservation to Checked Out. Folio balance should be settled first. Cancel Status: Reserved/Confirmed Cancels the reservation. Requires a cancellation reason. Waitlist — Moves to waitlist status. Print Registration Form Any Prints the guest registration form. Print Confirmation Letter Any Prints the booking confirmation letter. Open Billing In-House Opens the folio in Cashiering → Transaction.
From the reservation detail, you can print: Confirmation Letter — Sent to the guest to confirm the booking. Registration Form — Signed by the guest at check-in.

Reservations can belong to a Group. Group reservations share a group ID and group name. The first reservation in the group holds the deposit; subsequent group members do not require a separate deposit. Group management is available from the reservation list.

On the Booking Info step, click View Daily Rate to open the Daily Rate modal. This lets you set a custom rate for each individual night of the stay, overriding the flat rate code amount. The total is recalculated automatically.
Arrivals Departures Waitlist Cancellations Front Desk → Room Plan Cashiering → Transaction Housekeeping → Room Chart
This guide explains how to link an individual, company, or travel agent profile to a reservation — either as the primary guest, payer, or additional guest.
documents.s: Linking a Profile to a Reservation

documents.t: Linking profiles to reservations: Populates guest details automatically (name, contact, travel documents). Enables routing charges to a company or travel agent folio. Feeds the Guest Information Report for immigration compliance. Builds guest history for loyalty and repeat-guest tracking.

When creating or editing a reservation: Open the reservation form (new or existing). In Step 1 — Guest Info, use the Guest Search field. Type the guest's name, email, or phone number. Select the matching profile from the dropdown. The guest fields (name, email, phone, nationality, VIP level, travel documents) populate automatically. If no profile exists, fill in the fields manually — a new profile can be created from the reservation form or separately in Profile → Individual Profile.
documents.s: Linking an Individual Profile as Primary Guest

documents.t: A reservation can have multiple guests (e.g. a couple or family sharing a room). In Step 1 — Guest Info, click Add Another Guest. Search for and select an existing individual profile, or enter details manually. Repeat for each additional guest. All guests appear in the Guest Information Report.

To bill charges to a company account: In the reservation form Step 2 — Booking Info, find the Company or Payer field. Search for the company profile by name. Select it. The company is linked as the billing entity. Set up routing in Cashiering → Transaction (or Billing) to route room charges or other codes to the company folio automatically. See Room Routing for routing setup.
documents.s: Linking a Company Profile as Payer

documents.t: To associate a reservation with a travel agent (for commission tracking or AR billing): In the reservation form Step 2 — Booking Info, find the Travel Agent field. Search for the travel agent profile by name. Select it. The travel agent is linked to the reservation. Routing can be set up to send charges to the travel agent's AR account.
To change the guest or payer on an existing reservation: Open the reservation detail. Click Edit. Clear the current profile in the relevant field and search for the new one. Save.

If the guest does not have a profile yet: In the guest search field, type the name and press Enter (or click Create New). A quick-create form opens — fill in the required fields (name, email, phone). Save. The new profile is created and linked to the reservation. Complete the full profile later in Profile → Individual Profile to add travel documents and address.

The guest profile is not appearing in the search. Check that you are searching in the correct profile type (individual for guests, company for corporate accounts, travel agent for agencies). The profile must be active (not inactive/deactivated). Can I link both a company and a travel agent to the same reservation? Yes — a reservation can have a primary guest (individual), a company payer, and a travel agent all linked at the same time. The guest's travel documents are not showing in the Guest Information Report. Travel documents are stored on the individual profile (Step 3 — Travel Documents). Open the profile in Profile → Individual Profile and fill in the passport and visa fields.

Individual Profile — Create and manage guest profiles. Company Profile — Create and manage company profiles. Travel Agent Profile — Create and manage travel agent profiles. Reservations — Full reservation management. Room Routing — Route charges to company or travel agent. Guest Information Report — In-house guest list with travel document data.

Follow these steps in order to see the main flow of the PMS: login → dashboard → one reservation → one check-in → one folio. Use this path when you are new to the system or training staff. :::tip More detail Each step links to the full doc. For login, navigation, and “By role”, see Introduction. For end-to-end flows, see Workflows. :::
Open the PMS in your browser. Enter your username and password. Click Login. You are taken to the Dashboard. See Introduction → Logging in for details and screenshots.

After login you land on the Dashboard. From here you can: See occupancy, revenue, and housekeeping summary Use quick links into reservations, room plan, and other modules Spend a moment looking at the widgets and quick actions. See Dashboard for full detail.

Go to Reservation in the sidebar. Either create a new reservation (guest, dates, room type/room, rate) or find an existing reservation for today’s arrival. Make sure it is Confirmed (or equivalent) and has a room (or room type) and rate. See Reservations and Reservation list for creating and editing reservations.

Ensure the room is ready: in Housekeeping, the room should be Vacant Clean (or Ready). Change status if needed. Open the reservation (from Reservation or Front Desk → Room plan or In-House). Use Check in. The reservation becomes In-house (or Checked in). The guest is now in-house; charges can post to their folio. See Front Desk, Room plan, and Workflows → Check-in for the full check-in flow.

For Cashiering, log in to a cash register first if prompted (Cash Register Login). Go to Cashiering → Transaction. Select the reservation you just checked in. Its guest folio opens. Optionally add a charge (e.g. room, tax) or post a payment (e.g. deposit). Save. You have now used: login → dashboard → reservation → check-in → folio in one short path. See Cashiering and Transaction (Guest Folio) for daily cashier and folio work.
